drmd:description.dcc:content: Reference material BAM-U021a was obtained by blending soil from a contaminated site in Bremen, Germany with a TPH free sandy loamy soil sampled near Berlin, Germany. After air-drying, sieving and homogenisation, the fraction < 125 µm was subdivided into units of 71 g which were filled in amber glass bottles with screw caps equipped with PTFE-inserts and sealed with shrinking foil. The material is stored at BAM at -20 °C until dispatch. Details on the preparation and characterisation procedures are specified in the certification report.

1) Unweighted mean value of 11 laboratory means. 2) Estimated expanded uncertainty U with a coverage factor of k = 2, corresponding to a level of confidence of approx. 95%, as defined in the Guide to the expression of uncertainty in measurement, (GUM, ISO/IEC Guide 98-3:2008) [1].

drmd:intendedUse.dcc:content: BAM-U021a is explicitly meant only to be used in analytical laboratories. The intended purpose of reference material BAM-U021a is the verification of analytical procedures equivalent to ISO 16703:2011 [2,3] for the determination of the TPH content in soils and sediments by GC-FID.

drmd:storageInformation.dcc:content: BAM-U021a can be shipped at ambient temperature. Upon receipt the material must be stored at -20 °C in its original bottle. Before taking a subsample the bottle must have reached ambient temperature. Thereafter, the bottle must be closed tightly and stored at -20 °C. The stability of the reference material is not affected by short periods of handling at ambient temperature during transport and use.

drmd:instructionsForHandlingAndUse.dcc:content: It is strongly recommended to handle and dispose of the reference material in accordance with the guidelines for analytical soil samples legally in force at the site of end use and disposal.

drmd:metrologicalTraceability.dcc:content: The total petroleum hydrocarbon content is defined by the method employed for its determination. The certified value is the mass fraction of mineral oil obtained by the analytical procedure according to ISO 16703:2011 in relation to the certified calibration standard BAM-K010g. Thus, the stated references for BAM-U021a are ISO 16703:2011 and the calibration standard BAM-K010g mentioned for this purpose therein.

drmd:subcontractors.dcc:content: Analysen Service GmbH, Privates Institut für Umweltanalytik; Analysen Service GmbH, Umwelt- und Öllabor Leipzig; Bundesanstalt für Materialforschung und -prüfung (BAM); chemlab; Eurofins Umwelt Ost GmbH; Landeskriminalamt Berlin; Landeskriminalamt Brandenburg; NIUTEC AG; SGS Institut Fresenius GmbH; SYNLAB A&S Germany GmbH; WESSLING GmbH

drmd:referenceToCertificationReport.dcc:content: A detailed technical report describing the analytical procedures and the treatment of the analytical data used to certify BAM-U021a is available on request or can be downloaded from the BAM website (www.bam.de).

drmd:legalNotice.dcc:content: Neither BAM, its contractors nor any legal person acting on their behalf: (a) Make any warranty or representation, express or implied, that the use of any information, material, apparatus, method or process disclosed in this document does not infringe any privately owned intellectual property rights; or (b) Assume any liability with respect to, or for damages resulting from the use of any information, material, apparatus, method or process disclosed in this document.

drmd:digitalReferenceMaterialDocument.drmd:comment: For additional information please refer to the pdf certificate
